title: Vocabulary
objective: Students will explore vocabulary concepts including Defining Words, Root Words & Inflections.

steps.content: Let's explore Defining Words! Have you ever wondered about this?
Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., 'a duck is a bird that swims'), making real-life connections between words and their use
Let me show you something cool. Define 'tiger' as 'a large cat with stripes' — naming category and key attribute
Can you explain what a word means by saying what type of thing it is and what makes it special — for example, "a penguin is a bird that can't fly but is great at swimming"?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Defining Words: Define 'tiger' as 'a large cat with stripes' — naming category and key attribute Connect the word 'cozy' to real-life examples: places at home that feel cozy
Have you ever thought about Root Words & Inflections? Let's find out together!

Identify frequently occurring root words and their inflectional forms (e.g., look/looks/looked/looking), using affixes as clues to word meaning and understanding how suffixes create nouns and adjectives
Let me show you something cool. Identify 'play' as the root in 'played', 'playing', 'player'
When you sees words like "play," "plays," "played," and "playing," do they understand they all come from the same root word and can they use each form correctly?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Root Words & Inflections. Identify 'play' as the root in 'played', 'playing', 'player' Explain that '-ful' in 'careful' means 'full of care'
